title: AgentCo-op: Retrieval-Based Synthesis of Interoperable Multi-Agent Workflows
optimized_headline: "Discover AgentCo-op: A New Approach to Interoperable Multi-Agent Workflows"

raw_body:
arXiv:2605.20425v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Designing multi-agent workflows is especially difficult in open-ended scientific settings where tasks lack curated training sets, reliable scalar evaluation metrics, and standardized interfaces between existing tools and agents. We propose AgentCo-op, a retrieval-based synthesis framework that composes reusable skills, tools, and external agents into executable workflows through typed artifact handoffs, then applies bounded self-guided local repair to implicated components when execution evidence indicates failure. In two open-world genomics case studies, AgentCo-op composes independently developed scientific agents and external tool repositories into auditable workflows without redesigning them or running global topology search. It coordinates specialized agents for spatial transcriptomics and gene-set interpretation to enable collaborative discovery from spatial transcriptomics data, and builds a parallel workflow for cross-modality marker analysis on single-cell multiome data. AgentCo-op can also import a searched workflow as a structural prior and improve it by grounding nodes with retrieved components and applying local repair, showing that synthesis and search are complementary. On six coding, math, and question-answering benchmarks, AgentCo-op achieves the best result on four benchmarks and the best average score under a unified backbone setting, while consistently reducing per-task cost relative to multi-agent baselines. Together, these results suggest that retrieval-based synthesis can extend automated agentic workflow design beyond benchmark-optimized agent graphs to open-world workflows built from existing agents, tools, and typed artifacts.
speedrun: AgentCo-op is a new framework designed to create multi-agent workflows in complex scientific environments. It efficiently combines reusable skills and tools into functional workflows and can adapt when issues arise during execution. In tests, it outperformed existing models on four out of six benchmarks, reducing costs for each task. This innovation could significantly enhance collaborative scientific discovery by streamlining how agents and tools interact in open-ended scenarios.
why_it_matters: Researchers and scientists could benefit immediately by using AgentCo-op to simplify complex workflows and improve collaboration.
This development signals a shift towards more adaptable and efficient AI systems in scientific research, potentially accelerating discoveries.

lenses.eli12: AgentCo-op is like a smart assistant that helps different tools and agents work together seamlessly, even when tasks are complicated. It can fix problems on the fly, making sure everything runs smoothly. This matters because it could help scientists collaborate more effectively and make discoveries faster.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, AgentCo-op presents a way to streamline complex workflows, addressing user needs for efficiency in scientific research. By reducing task costs and improving collaboration, it could enhance product offerings in the AI and research sectors, leading to better user satisfaction and faster results.
lenses.engineer: From a technical standpoint, AgentCo-op utilizes a retrieval-based synthesis approach, combining various agents and tools without needing a complete redesign. It has shown superior performance on benchmarks, achieving the best results on four out of six tests while lowering costs compared to traditional multi-agent systems. This suggests its potential for scalable and efficient workflow design in diverse applications.
